BINGO! That's the bug!

`defaults.items()` where `defaults` is `Namespace(a=Namespace(x=1))` yields `[('a.x', 1)]` instead of `[('a', Namespace(x=1))]`.

This is because `Namespace.items()` by default uses `branches=False`, which flattens nested namespaces.

So in `add_class_arguments`:
```python
defaults = default.lazy_get_init_args()  # Returns Namespace(a=Namespace(x=1))
if defaults:
    defaults = {prefix + k: v for k, v in defaults.items() if k not in skip}  # Yields {"a.x": 1}
    self.set_defaults(**defaults)  # Becomes set_defaults(a.x=1)
```

This explains exactly why `set_defaults` is being called with `x=1` instead of `a={"x": 1}`.

The fix is to make sure that when we iterate over the defaults from `lazy_get_init_args()`, we use `branches=True` to get the non-flattened items.
